name: turbodbc (High-Performance ODBC for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Blue Yonder / JDA Software

auth.notes: ODBC connection strings; inherits driver auth (Windows Auth, SQL Auth, etc.)

performance.notes: Arrow-based bulk transfer; 10-100x faster than pyodbc for large result sets

agent_readiness.notes: High-performance ODBC wrapper using Apache Arrow for bulk data transfer. from turbodbc import connect. conn = connect(driver='ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server', server='...', database='...'). cursor = conn.cursor(). cursor.execute(sql). Arrow transfer: cursor.fetchallarrow(). pandas: cursor.fetchallnumpy() or via Arrow. 10-100x faster than pyodbc for bulk reads. Turbodbc bulk INSERT via executemanycolumns(). C++ ODBC layer. Requires unixODBC on Linux. SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Teradata.

gotchas: More complex installation than pyodbc (requires Arrow, C++ deps)
Not all ODBC operations supported (focus on bulk SELECT/INSERT)
Debugging Arrow type mismatches can be difficult
best_when: Bulk data extraction from SQL Server/Teradata to pandas/Arrow
When pyodbc fetch performance is a bottleneck
High-throughput ETL pipelines reading large SQL result sets
Zero-copy Arrow data transfer from database to DataFrame
avoid_when: Small queries where pyodbc overhead is negligible
When native drivers (psycopg2, asyncpg) are available
Complex ODBC operations beyond bulk SELECT/INSERT
summary: turbodbc wraps ODBC with Apache Arrow for 10-100x faster bulk data transfer vs pyodbc. cursor.fetchallarrow() returns Apache Arrow batches for zero-copy pandas loading. executemanycolumns() for bulk INSERT. C++ backend. Use for high-throughput SQL Server/Teradata ETL where pyodbc is the bottleneck. More complex install than pyodbc but significant performance gain for large result sets.
